description:
You are assisting a researcher who is tidying up their working directory.  
At the moment the researcher already has a folder **/home/user/raw_data** that contains two CSV
files generated by laboratory equipment.  
Your job is to create an easy-to-remember *datasets* folder that exposes the
latest revision of each table via symbolic links, **and** to create a tiny log
so that an automated checker can verify the work.

Requested actions

1. Make sure the directory **/home/user/projects/datasets** exists (create the
   parent directory *projects* as well if it is missing).
2. Inside **/home/user/projects/datasets** create the following symbolic links  
   (exact names, letter case and extension matter):
   • dataset_A_latest.csv → /home/user/raw_data/dataset_A_v1.csv  
   • dataset_B_latest.csv → /home/user/raw_data/dataset_B_v1.csv
3. Produce a plain-text log file named **symlink_report.log** in
   **/home/user/projects/datasets** that lists, one per line, each link name
   followed by the string “ -> ” (space, hyphen, greater-than sign), and then
   the absolute path it points to.

The final **symlink_report.log** must contain exactly the two lines below,
terminated with a single UNIX newline each (no extra blank lines):

dataset_A_latest.csv -> /home/user/raw_data/dataset_A_v1.csv  
dataset_B_latest.csv -> /home/user/raw_data/dataset_B_v1.csv

Important notes for the automated test

• The paths must be absolute, match precisely what is shown above, and be in
  that order.  
• The log file’s name, location, and contents are case-sensitive.  
• The symbolic links themselves must exist and resolve to the expected target
  files.
truth:
Pre-existing files (already present before the agent starts):
1. /home/user/raw_data/dataset_A_v1.csv  
   Contents (2 lines, trailing newline on each):  
   time,signal  
   0,0.123  

2. /home/user/raw_data/dataset_B_v1.csv  
   Contents (2 lines, trailing newline on each):  
   time,value  
   0,42.7  

Expected artifacts after successful completion:

A. Directory:
   /home/user/projects/datasets  (permissions 0755, owned by user)

B. Symbolic links inside that directory:
   /home/user/projects/datasets/dataset_A_latest.csv  →  /home/user/raw_data/dataset_A_v1.csv  
   /home/user/projects/datasets/dataset_B_latest.csv  →  /home/user/raw_data/dataset_B_v1.csv  

C. Verification log:
   File: /home/user/projects/datasets/symlink_report.log  
   Contents (including trailing newlines, exactly two lines):
dataset_A_latest.csv -> /home/user/raw_data/dataset_A_v1.csv
dataset_B_latest.csv -> /home/user/raw_data/dataset_B_v1.csv

The automated checker will verify:
• Existence and correctness of both symbolic links (using readlink -f).  
• Exact byte-for-byte match of symlink_report.log to the string above.
name: task_000000_0fea8120
